DISCOVERY 302, MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND IN THE ALTERNATIVE REQUESTS FOR ISSUE AND/OR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM AUG-10-2017 TO AUG-30-2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPARTMENT 302, LAW & MOTION. PLAINTIFF IS INFORMED THAT A $30. COURT REPORTER FEE WILL BE ASSESSED AND MUST BE PAID BEFORE THE HEARING DATE. JUDGE PRO TEM TO PREPARE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-23-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO DEFENDANT ARLENE BROWN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF JOHN DIAMANTE'S CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. MR. DIAMANTE HAS NOT SHOWN THAT FACTS ESSENTIAL TO JUSTIFY OPPOSITION MAY EXIST BUT CANNOT BE PRESENTED, PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(H). THE PICTURE THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF THE MOTION TO COMPEL, EVEN IF PRODUCED, WOULD NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM. MS. BROWN HAS SATISFIED HER INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. MS. BROWN HAS SHOWN THAT SHE WAS NOT ILLEGALLY USING AN ELECTRONIC DEVICE IN VIOLATION OF VEHICLE CODE 23123 AND/OR 23123.5 AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT. (UMF 8.) MR. DIAMANTE FAILED TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE SINCE HE DECLINED TO FILE ANY SUBSTANTIVE OPPOSITION, INCLUDING A RESPONSE TO MS. BROWN'S SEPARATE STATEMENT, TO THE MOTION. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) MOVING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING, IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND CCP 437C(G). JUDGE: CHARLENE P. KIESSELBACH, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: GORDON AIAVAO, CSR #11216, GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (302/CPK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION OF DEFENDANT ARLENE BROWN TO STRIKE REQUEST FOR EXEMPLARY DAMAGES FROM COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT PLEADS SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEADLINE TO ANSWER IS ACCORDING TO STATUTE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT, CSR #4609, BUS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION By Defendant Arlene Brown Of Punitive Damages Claim; Separate Statement Of Undisputed Facts; Support Memoradum; And Supporting Evidence
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bryan Lamb, Esq. (415-997-9529)) for Plaintiff/Opposing party .
Lorraine Walsh, Esq. (925-825-9500) for Defendant/Moving party ARLENE BROWN.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues she did not receive proper notice re: contesting the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ARLENE BROWN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Punitive Damages Claim.

Defendant Arlene Brown's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff John Diamante's claim for punitive damages is granted. Mr. Diamante has not shown that facts essential to justify opposition may exist but cannot be presented, pursuant to CCP 437c(h). The picture that is the subject of the motion to compel, even if produced, would not create a triable issue as to the punitive damages claim. Ms. Brown has satisfied her initial summary judgment burden. Ms. Brown has shown that she was not illegally using an electronic device in violation of Vehicle Code 23123 and/or 23123.5 at the time of the accident. (UMF 8.) Mr. Diamante failed to raise a triable issue since he declined to file any substantive opposition, including a response to Ms. Brown's separate statement, to the motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling, in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437c(g). Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR #11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/CPK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Arlene Brown To Strike Request For Exemplary Damages From Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Genevieve K. Guertin, Esq. (415-683-0278) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Edward W. Hawkyard of Catherine A. Walsh Law Offices (925-825-9500) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

MOTION OF DEFENDANT ARLENE BROWN To Strike Request For Exemplary Damages From Complaint is DENIED. The complaint pleads sufficient facts to support a claim for punitive damages. Deadline to answer is according to statute. Written order is signed and filed in open court.
